complex: Hypertension is a chronic condition characterized by persistently elevated arterial blood pressure exceeding 140/90 mmHg, increasing risk of cardiovascular morbidity and mortality.
reference_simple: High blood pressure is when the force of blood against your artery walls stays too high for too long. A reading above 140/90 is considered high. It raises the risk of heart disease and stroke.

complex: The patient was diagnosed with type 2 diabetes mellitus with peripheral neuropathy and prescribed metformin 500mg twice daily with meals.
reference_simple: The patient has type 2 diabetes and nerve damage in their hands and feet. They were prescribed metformin, a diabetes medication, 500mg to be taken twice a day with food.

complex: Post-operative assessment reveals satisfactory wound healing with no signs of dehiscence or surgical site infection. Patient is advised to continue prophylactic antibiotics for 5 days.
reference_simple: After surgery, the wound is healing well with no signs of reopening or infection. The patient should continue taking preventive antibiotics for 5 days.

complex: The patient presented to the emergency department with acute myocardial infarction and underwent percutaneous coronary intervention with stent placement in the left anterior descending artery.
reference_simple: The patient came to the emergency room having a heart attack. Doctors opened a blocked artery in the heart and placed a small mesh tube, called a stent, to keep it open.

complex: Pulmonary function tests are consistent with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, demonstrating a reduced FEV1/FVC ratio indicative of airflow obstruction.
reference_simple: Breathing tests show the patient has COPD, a long-term lung disease. The tests show that air does not flow out of the lungs as easily as it should.

complex: Laboratory findings reveal elevated serum creatinine and reduced glomerular filtration rate, suggestive of impaired renal function requiring nephrology referral.
reference_simple: Blood tests show the kidneys are not filtering waste as well as they should. The patient will be referred to a kidney specialist for further care.

complex: The biopsy confirmed a malignant neoplasm of the colon with lymphovascular invasion; adjuvant chemotherapy is recommended following surgical resection.
reference_simple: A tissue sample confirmed cancer in the colon that has started to spread. After surgery to remove it, the patient should have chemotherapy to help stop it from coming back.

complex: Magnetic resonance imaging of the lumbar spine demonstrates a herniated intervertebral disc at L4-L5 with compression of the adjacent nerve root.
reference_simple: An MRI scan of the lower back shows a slipped disc between two bones of the spine. It is pressing on a nearby nerve, which can cause pain.

complex: The patient exhibits symptoms of hyperthyroidism secondary to Graves disease, including tachycardia, weight loss, and heat intolerance, and will commence antithyroid therapy.
reference_simple: The patient has an overactive thyroid caused by a condition called Graves disease. This makes the heart beat fast, causes weight loss, and makes them feel too hot. They will start medicine to calm the thyroid.

complex: Echocardiography revealed a reduced left ventricular ejection fraction of 35%, consistent with systolic heart failure; the patient was initiated on an ACE inhibitor and beta-blocker.
reference_simple: A heart ultrasound showed the heart is pumping out only 35% of its blood with each beat, which is low. This is a type of heart failure. The patient was started on two medicines to help the heart work better.

complex: The dermatological examination is consistent with psoriasis vulgaris, presenting as well-demarcated erythematous plaques with silvery scale over the extensor surfaces.
reference_simple: The skin exam shows psoriasis, a long-term skin condition. It causes red, raised patches covered with silvery flakes, often on the elbows and knees.

complex: Cerebrospinal fluid analysis indicates bacterial meningitis; empiric intravenous antibiotic therapy should be administered without delay.
reference_simple: Tests on the fluid around the brain and spine show a serious infection called bacterial meningitis. The patient needs antibiotics given through a vein right away.

complex: The patient sustained a comminuted fracture of the distal radius requiring open reduction and internal fixation with a volar locking plate.
reference_simple: The patient broke their wrist bone into several pieces. Surgeons had to put the pieces back in place and hold them together with a metal plate and screws.

complex: Upper endoscopy revealed a gastric ulcer with associated Helicobacter pylori infection; triple therapy with a proton pump inhibitor and two antibiotics was initiated.
reference_simple: A camera test of the stomach found an ulcer caused by a germ called H. pylori. The patient was started on three medicines: one to lower stomach acid and two antibiotics to clear the germ.

complex: The neonate presented with hyperbilirubinemia requiring phototherapy to reduce the risk of kernicterus.
reference_simple: The newborn baby had too much of a yellow substance called bilirubin in the blood, causing jaundice. The baby was treated with special lights to lower it and prevent brain damage.

complex: Coronary angiography demonstrated significant stenosis of the right coronary artery, and the patient was scheduled for coronary artery bypass grafting.
reference_simple: A special X-ray of the heart's blood vessels showed a major blockage in one artery. The patient is scheduled for bypass surgery to route blood around the blockage.

complex: The patient was diagnosed with rheumatoid arthritis and started on a disease-modifying antirheumatic drug to slow joint destruction.
reference_simple: The patient has rheumatoid arthritis, a condition where the immune system attacks the joints. They were started on a medicine that slows down damage to the joints.

complex: Computed tomography of the abdomen revealed acute appendicitis with periappendiceal fluid, necessitating urgent laparoscopic appendectomy.
reference_simple: A CT scan of the belly showed the appendix was inflamed with fluid around it. The patient needs urgent keyhole surgery to remove the appendix.

complex: The patient's hemoglobin A1c of 9.2% indicates poorly controlled diabetes, and insulin therapy will be intensified.
reference_simple: A blood test shows the patient's average blood sugar has been too high over the past few months. Their diabetes is not well controlled, so their insulin treatment will be increased.

complex: Spirometry confirmed a diagnosis of asthma with significant reversibility following bronchodilator administration.
reference_simple: A breathing test confirmed the patient has asthma. Their breathing got much better after using an inhaler that opens the airways.

complex: The patient presented with an acute ischemic stroke and received intravenous thrombolysis within the therapeutic window.
reference_simple: The patient had a stroke caused by a blood clot blocking blood flow to the brain. They were given a clot-busting medicine through a vein in time to help.

complex: Histopathology of the breast lesion confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma, estrogen receptor positive, and the patient will undergo lumpectomy followed by radiotherapy.
reference_simple: Tests on the breast lump confirmed a type of breast cancer that grows in response to the hormone estrogen. The patient will have surgery to remove the lump, followed by radiation treatment.

complex: The patient exhibits signs of dehydration secondary to acute gastroenteritis and requires intravenous fluid resuscitation.
reference_simple: The patient is dehydrated because of a stomach bug causing vomiting and diarrhea. They need fluids given through a vein to replace what they have lost.

complex: Bone densitometry revealed osteoporosis with a T-score of -2.8, and bisphosphonate therapy was recommended to reduce fracture risk.
reference_simple: A bone scan showed the patient has osteoporosis, meaning their bones are weak and thin. They were advised to take a medicine that strengthens bones and lowers the chance of breaks.

complex: The patient was found to have atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response and was started on anticoagulation to prevent thromboembolic events.
reference_simple: The patient has an irregular, fast heartbeat called atrial fibrillation. They were started on a blood thinner to prevent clots that could cause a stroke.

complex: Ophthalmologic examination revealed proliferative diabetic retinopathy requiring panretinal photocoagulation to preserve vision.
reference_simple: An eye exam showed diabetes has damaged the blood vessels in the back of the eye. The patient needs laser treatment to protect their eyesight.

complex: The patient presented with cholelithiasis and recurrent biliary colic; elective laparoscopic cholecystectomy was advised.
reference_simple: The patient has gallstones that keep causing bouts of pain. Doctors advised planned keyhole surgery to remove the gallbladder.

complex: Serologic testing confirmed acute hepatitis B infection, and the patient was counseled regarding transmission and monitoring of liver function.
reference_simple: Blood tests confirmed the patient recently caught hepatitis B, a virus that affects the liver. They were told how it spreads and that their liver will be checked regularly.

complex: The patient was diagnosed with major depressive disorder and initiated on a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor with scheduled follow-up.
reference_simple: The patient was diagnosed with depression and started on an antidepressant medicine. A follow-up visit was scheduled to see how they are doing.

complex: Renal ultrasound identified hydronephrosis secondary to an obstructing ureteral calculus, and urologic intervention is planned.
reference_simple: A kidney ultrasound found that a stone is blocking the tube from the kidney, causing urine to back up and swell the kidney. A urine-system specialist will treat it.

complex: The patient developed community-acquired pneumonia with a consolidation in the right lower lobe on chest radiograph and was started on empiric antibiotics.
reference_simple: A chest X-ray showed the patient has pneumonia, a lung infection, in the lower part of the right lung. They were started on antibiotics.

complex: The patient's lipid panel revealed hypercholesterolemia with elevated low-density lipoprotein, warranting initiation of statin therapy.
reference_simple: A blood test showed the patient has high cholesterol, including high levels of the 'bad' type. They should start a statin, a medicine that lowers cholesterol.

complex: Colonoscopy revealed multiple adenomatous polyps, which were removed via polypectomy and sent for histopathologic evaluation.
reference_simple: During a colonoscopy, doctors found several growths in the colon. They removed them and sent them to a lab to check whether they were harmful.

complex: The patient sustained a traumatic brain injury with a subdural hematoma requiring emergent surgical evacuation.
reference_simple: The patient had a serious head injury causing bleeding between the skull and the brain. They needed emergency surgery to remove the blood and relieve pressure.

complex: The patient was diagnosed with hypothyroidism and started on levothyroxine with periodic monitoring of thyroid-stimulating hormone.
reference_simple: The patient has an underactive thyroid. They were started on a thyroid hormone pill, and their thyroid levels will be checked from time to time.

complex: Arthrocentesis of the knee yielded fluid consistent with gout, revealing monosodium urate crystals under polarized microscopy.
reference_simple: Doctors drew fluid from the swollen knee and found it was caused by gout. Under a microscope, they saw the crystals that cause this painful joint condition.

complex: The patient presented with anaphylaxis following exposure to a known allergen and was treated with intramuscular epinephrine.
reference_simple: The patient had a severe, life-threatening allergic reaction after contact with something they are allergic to. They were treated with an epinephrine injection into the muscle.

complex: Fundoscopic examination revealed papilledema, raising concern for elevated intracranial pressure requiring further neuroimaging.
reference_simple: An eye exam showed swelling at the back of the eye, which can be a sign of increased pressure inside the skull. More brain scans are needed to find the cause.

complex: The patient was diagnosed with deep vein thrombosis of the left lower extremity and commenced on therapeutic anticoagulation.
reference_simple: The patient has a blood clot in a deep vein of the left leg. They were started on blood-thinning medicine to treat it and prevent it from getting worse.

complex: The patient's electrocardiogram demonstrated ST-segment elevation, prompting activation of the cardiac catheterization laboratory.
reference_simple: A heart tracing showed a pattern that means a heart attack is happening. The team was called to quickly open the blocked artery in the heart.

complex: The patient presented with nephrotic syndrome characterized by proteinuria, hypoalbuminemia, and peripheral edema.
reference_simple: The patient has a kidney problem that lets too much protein leak into the urine. This lowers protein in the blood and causes swelling in the body.

complex: The patient was diagnosed with benign prostatic hyperplasia and started on an alpha-blocker to relieve urinary obstruction.
reference_simple: The patient has an enlarged prostate, which is not cancer but blocks the flow of urine. They were started on a medicine that relaxes the area to make urinating easier.

complex: Skin biopsy confirmed malignant melanoma with a Breslow thickness of 1.2mm, and wide local excision was recommended.
reference_simple: A skin sample confirmed melanoma, a serious skin cancer, that has grown a small distance into the skin. Doctors recommended surgery to remove it along with a margin of healthy skin.

complex: The patient experienced a seizure and electroencephalography revealed epileptiform discharges; antiepileptic medication was initiated.
reference_simple: The patient had a seizure, and a brain test showed unusual electrical activity linked to epilepsy. They were started on medicine to prevent future seizures.

complex: The patient was found to have iron deficiency anemia and was prescribed oral iron supplementation with dietary counseling.
reference_simple: Tests showed the patient has anemia caused by low iron. They were given iron pills to take by mouth and advice on eating iron-rich foods.

complex: Transvaginal ultrasound confirmed an ectopic pregnancy in the right fallopian tube, and methotrexate therapy was administered.
reference_simple: An ultrasound confirmed the pregnancy was growing in the fallopian tube instead of the womb, which is dangerous. The patient was given a medicine called methotrexate to end it safely.

complex: The patient presented with cellulitis of the lower limb exhibiting erythema, warmth, and tenderness, and was started on oral antibiotics.
reference_simple: The patient has a skin infection in the leg that is red, warm, and sore to touch. They were started on antibiotic pills to clear it.

complex: The patient was diagnosed with gastroesophageal reflux disease and advised on lifestyle modification alongside a proton pump inhibitor.
reference_simple: The patient has acid reflux, where stomach acid flows back up and causes heartburn. They were given lifestyle tips and a medicine that reduces stomach acid.

complex: Audiometry confirmed sensorineural hearing loss, and the patient was referred for hearing aid evaluation.
reference_simple: A hearing test confirmed permanent hearing loss caused by damage to the inner ear or nerve. The patient was referred to be assessed for hearing aids.

complex: The patient exhibited signs of sepsis with hypotension and elevated lactate, requiring aggressive fluid resuscitation and broad-spectrum antibiotics.
reference_simple: The patient showed signs of sepsis, a dangerous body-wide response to infection, with low blood pressure. They needed large amounts of fluids and strong antibiotics quickly.
